source:
# 🧬 Project 14: Gene Expression Time Series
### I 320D – Data Science for Biomedical Informatics | UT Austin
---
**Goal:** Cluster longitudinal RNA-seq data from healthy adults inoculated with H3N2 influenza to find temporal gene clusters.

**Notebook structure (Adapted from Project 4 Sepsis template):**

| Step | Topic |
|------|-------|
| 0 | Environment setup & data download |
| 1 | Data loading & first look |
| 2 | Exploratory data analysis (EDA) |
| 3 | Missing data & imputation |
| 4 | Feature engineering (Decoder Ring / Alignment Map) |
| 5 | Train / test split (Filtering Top Variable Genes) |
| 6 | Baseline model – Hierarchical Clustering |
| 7 | Temporal model – K-Means Trajectories |
| 8 | Evaluation (Cluster Cohesion) |
| 9 | Interpretation (GO Pathway Enrichment) |
| 10 | Discussion & reflection questions |

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

print("✅ Environment setup complete.")

---
## Step 1 | Data loading & first look
> **Note:** We are exclusively using GSE30550 for the expression matrix because it contains the hourly timepoints required by the rubric. SDY212 only contains Day 0 and Day 28.

file_path = 'GSE30550_series_matrix.txt'

# 1. Extract Sample Names from the text file header
sample_names = []
with open(file_path, 'r') as file:
    for line in file:
        if line.startswith('!Sample_title'):
            sample_names = [name.strip('\"\n') for name in line.split('\t')[1:]]
            break

# 2. Load the actual expression matrix
gse_expr = pd.read_csv(file_path, sep='\t', comment='!', index_col=0)
gse_expr.columns = sample_names

print("✅ GSE30550 Loaded successfully.")
display(gse_expr.head(3))

---
## Step 2 | Exploratory data analysis (EDA)
Exploring shapes, data types, missing values, duplicates, and statistics.

print("="*50 + "\n🔬 GSE30550 EXPRESSION EDA\n" + "="*50)

print("📐 Shape:", gse_expr.shape)
print(f"\n📋 Columns: {len(gse_expr.columns)} columns (Samples)")
print(f"🧬 Data Types: {gse_expr.dtypes.nunique()} unique data type(s) -> {gse_expr.dtypes.unique()}")
print("\n💾 Memory Usage:", round(gse_expr.memory_usage(deep=True).sum() / 1024**2, 2), "MB")
print(f"\n⚠️ Total Missing Values: {gse_expr.isnull().sum().sum():,}")
print(f"👯 Total Duplicate Rows (Genes): {gse_expr.duplicated().sum():,}")

print("\n📊 Statistics (First 5 samples only to save space):")
display(gse_expr.iloc[:, :5].describe())

print("\n👟 Last Look (Tail):")
display(gse_expr.tail(3))

---
## Step 3 | Missing data & imputation
> **Note:** GEO files often contain a trailing tab at the end of every row, which causes Pandas to generate a 'ghost column' of NaNs. We will drop this, along with any rows containing missing technical reads.

print("Before cleanup:", gse_expr.shape)

# 1. Drop the 'Ghost Column' (removes columns where ALL values are NaN)
gse_expr = gse_expr.dropna(axis=1, how='all')

# 2. Drop any rows (genes) with missing expression values
gse_expr = gse_expr.dropna(axis=0, how='any')

print("After cleanup:", gse_expr.shape)
print("\n✅ Missing data handled. Expression matrix is clean.")

---
## Step 4 | Feature engineering (Decoder Ring / Alignment Map)
We extract the clinical metadata from the GSE30550 header and harmonize it into clean, usable features (Subject, Hour, Outcome).
# 1. Extract the raw metadata lines from the GEO text file
# Convert to DataFrame and transpose so samples are rows
cols_to_keep = [col for col in metadata_df.columns if 'title' in col or 'characteristics_ch1' in col]
